refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plans and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that conflates productivity measurement with operational capability. Refinement separates the functional requirement (enable task execution at target productivity) from the verification criterion (measured KPI ≥ target). This clarifies that productivity is an emergent property dependent on vehicle capability, operator effectiveness (on-site or remote), task planning, and safety constraints. High safety criticality requires explicit safety integration into the functional decomposition.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure actual productivity (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations under controlled conditions; cross-reference against task logs, telemetry records, and safety event logs to ensure no safety violations masked the measurement.
sub_frs: FR-1.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving operations (dig, load, transport, dump) with specified cycle time and payload capacity.
FR-1.2: The operator interface (on-site or remote) shall provide sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to command the vehicle safely and effectively.
FR-1.3: The NGHE solution shall enforce safety constraints (geofencing, speed limits, load limits, emergency stop) during all operations.
FR-1.4: The NGHE solution shall log and report productivity metrics (cycle count, material moved, time per cycle) for post-operation analysis.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate a high-payload, low-cycle-time vehicle with real-time telemetry and remote control capability; enforce safety rules via software interlocks; measure productivity via onboard sensors and log aggregation.
DP-B: Deploy a vehicle with baseline earthmoving capability; rely on operator skill and manual safety compliance; measure productivity via manual timekeeping and post-operation reporting.

DP-C: Implement a hybrid approach: vehicle with moderate payload and cycle time; operator-assisted safety (alerts and recommendations); telemetry-based productivity measurement with periodic manual verification.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Integrate a high-payload, low-cycle-time vehicle with real-time telemetry and remote control capability; enforce safety rules via software interlocks; measure productivity via onboard sensors and log aggregation.

potential_sources: Vehicle mechanical design (payload, cycle time) is coupled to operator interface responsiveness (latency, control fidelity) — both must be co-optimized.
Safety interlocks (software rules) are coupled to telemetry accuracy — poor sensor data leads to unsafe rule enforcement.
Productivity measurement depends on sensor calibration and log integrity — decoupling measurement from vehicle operation risks data loss.
coupling_check.notes: Couplings are manageable via interface contracts (telemetry schema, safety rule specification, measurement protocol). No fundamental design contradiction detected. DP-A is internally consistent and addresses all sub-FRs.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Vehicle mechanical design can achieve target cycle time and payload within cost and weight constraints.
Assumption: Teleoperation network (latency, bandwidth) can support safe remote operation.
Assumption: Onboard sensors (load cells, position encoders, GNSS) are reliable and calibrated.
Risk: If vehicle cycle time cannot be reduced below current baseline, productivity target may be unachievable regardless of operator skill.
Risk: If teleoperation latency exceeds operator reaction time, remote operation may be unsafe or ineffective.
Risk: Sensor drift or calibration errors could invalidate productivity measurements and mask safety violations.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-A to explicitly specify target cycle time, payload capacity, telemetry latency budget, and sensor accuracy requirements. Add a fallback DP-B (manual operation with safety alerts) for scenarios where remote operation is infeasible.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The top-level system boundary encompassing all vehicle, teleoperation, safety, and measurement subsystems. Owns the integration of vehicle operations, remote control, safety enforcement, and productivity logging. Interfaces with external worksite systems (task dispatch, safety management) and operators (on-site and remote).

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives task definitions (dig location, load target, dump location, priority) from site manager or task planning system.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Transmits real-time vehicle state (position, payload, cycle status, sensor readings) to operator interface and safety monitoring system.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Broadcasts safety events (geofence breach, overload, emergency stop, anomaly) to safety manager and operator.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional communication channel for teleoperation commands (operator to vehicle) and telemetry/safety data (vehicle to operator and safety services).

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level boundary and naturally contains the entire productivity requirement. All sub-functions (vehicle operation, teleoperation, safety, measurement) are owned within this module. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross the boundary.

Vehicle module owns mechanical and electrical subsystems (engine, hydraulics, sensors, actuators) that directly enable cycle time and payload. However, productivity is not solely a vehicle property—it depends on operator control, safety constraints, and task planning. Assigning FR-NGHE-001 to Vehicle alone would diffuse responsibility for teleoperation, safety, and measurement.

Teleoperation module owns remote control interface and network communication. It enables operator effectiveness but does not own vehicle capability, safety enforcement, or measurement. Assigning productivity FR to Teleoperation alone would obscure vehicle performance and safety constraints.

Safety Services owns safety rule enforcement and monitoring. While safety constraints affect achievable productivity, Safety Services does not own vehicle capability or measurement. Assigning productivity FR to Safety Services would create confusion about primary responsibility.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The productivity requirement is a system-level emergent property that depends on the integrated behavior of vehicle, teleoperation, safety, and measurement subsystems. No single subsystem module can own this requirement in isolation. The NGHE Solution module, as the system-level boundary, is the appropriate owner. Sub-FRs (vehicle cycle time, operator interface responsiveness, safety constraint enforcement, measurement accuracy) are then allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and a measurement/logging subsystem respectively.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution is treated as a passive container rather than an active integrator, responsibility for cross-module optimization (e.g., balancing safety constraints against productivity) may be lost.

Risk: Interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) must be precisely specified to ensure sub-modules coordinate correctly. Ambiguous interface contracts could lead to integration failures.

Risk: Measurement subsystem (productivity logging) is not explicitly listed as a candidate module. If measurement is diffused across Vehicle and Teleoperation, data integrity and traceability may suffer.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Confirm that a measurement/logging subsystem exists or will be created within NGHE Solution to own FR-1.4 (productivity metrics logging). Ensure interface contracts for Telemetry and SafetyAlert are detailed enough to support both real-time safety enforcement and post-operation productivity analysis. Consider adding a 'Measurement' or 'Data Management' module to the available modules list.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (integrated vehicle with teleoperation, safety interlocks, and telemetry-based measurement) directly addresses the refined FR (enable task execution at target productivity while maintaining safety compliance). The DP specifies a coherent solution approach: high-performance vehicle + remote control + software safety enforcement + sensor-based measurement. No logical gaps or contradictions detected.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk 1: Productivity measurement (DP-A component) is assigned to NGHE Solution but may be implemented across Vehicle (sensor acquisition), Teleoperation (data transmission), and a logging subsystem (data storage/analysis). Interface contracts must ensure data flows correctly and measurement integrity is maintained.

Diffusion Risk 2: Safety interlocks (DP-A component) are assigned to Safety Services module, but enforcement logic may be distributed across Vehicle (actuator control), Teleoperation (command validation), and Safety Services (rule engine). Coordination via Network Link and SafetyAlert interfaces is critical.

Diffusion Risk 3: Teleoperation capability (DP-A component) depends on Network Link quality (latency, bandwidth). If Network Link is owned by external infrastructure, NGHE Solution has limited control over a critical performance factor.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate system-level owner. Sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are well-positioned to own their respective sub-FRs. External interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are clearly defined. Containment is sound if sub-module responsibilities are explicitly allocated in the next decomposition level.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Productivity measurement is feasible via onboard sensors (load cells, cycle counters) and telemetry logging. Safety compliance can be verified via safety event logs and geofence/constraint audit trails. Operator effectiveness can be assessed via task completion time and error rates. Verification plan should include controlled trials with representative tasks and worksite conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: FR-NGHE-001 is allocated to NGHE Solution as the system-level owner. Sub-FRs are allocated as follows: FR-1.1 (vehicle cycle time/payload) → Vehicle module; FR-1.2 (operator interface) → Teleoperation module; FR-1.3 (safety constraints) → Safety Services module; FR-1.4 (measurement) → NGHE Solution (with implementation across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and a logging subsystem). Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Revision 1: Explicitly define sub-module responsibilities and interface contracts in the next decomposition level. Ensure Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules understand their role in achieving the system-level productivity FR.

Revision 2: Specify measurement subsystem ownership and data flow. Clarify how sensor data from Vehicle flows through Network Link to a logging/analysis system, and how measurement integrity is assured.

Revision 3: Define Network Link performance requirements (latency, bandwidth, reliability) to support both teleoperation and safety-critical data transmission. Identify external dependencies and mitigation strategies.
Revision 4: Add acceptance criteria for each sub-FR (e.g., vehicle cycle time ≤ X seconds, teleoperation latency ≤ Y ms, safety constraint enforcement ≥ Z% accuracy) to enable verification.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-001 is a system-level acceptance criterion that is not atomic. It depends on multiple sub-systems (vehicle, teleoperation, safety, measurement) and their integration. The refined FR and selected DP are sound, and the assignment to NGHE Solution is appropriate. However, the requirement cannot be fully verified or implemented at this level. Decomposition into sub-FRs (FR-1.1 through FR-1.4) is necessary to allocate responsibility to specific modules and enable detailed design. Proceed to the next decomposition level for Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Measurement subsystems.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-1.1: The Vehicle module shall execute earthmoving operations (dig, load, transport, dump) with cycle time ≤ [target_cycle_time] seconds and payload capacity ≥ [target_payload] tons.

FR-1.2: The Teleoperation module shall provide operator control and situational awareness with latency ≤ [target_latency] ms and command fidelity ≥ [target_fidelity] % for safe and effective remote operation.

FR-1.3: The Safety Services module shall enforce geofencing, speed limits, load limits, and emergency stop with constraint violation detection ≥ [target_detection_rate] % and false positive rate ≤ [target_false_positive_rate] %.

FR-1.4: The NGHE Solution measurement subsystem shall log productivity metrics (cycle count, material moved, time per cycle) with data accuracy ≥ [target_accuracy] % and availability ≥ [target_availability] % for post-operation analysis.

assumptions: Assumption 1: Vehicle mechanical design can achieve target cycle time and payload within cost and weight constraints.
Assumption 2: Teleoperation network (latency, bandwidth) can support safe remote operation with acceptable operator workload.
Assumption 3: Onboard sensors (load cells, position encoders, GNSS) are reliable, calibrated, and provide sufficient accuracy for productivity measurement.
Assumption 4: Safety interlocks can be implemented in software without introducing new failure modes or reducing system availability.
Assumption 5: Operator skill and training are sufficient to achieve target productivity with the provided interface and vehicle capability.
Assumption 6: Worksite conditions (terrain, material properties, task complexity) are representative of the evaluation period and do not introduce uncontrolled variability.
open_risks: Risk 1: Vehicle cycle time may be limited by mechanical constraints (hydraulic flow, actuator speed) and may not achieve target productivity regardless of operator skill.
Risk 2: Teleoperation latency may exceed acceptable limits due to network congestion or infrastructure limitations, making remote operation unsafe or ineffective.
Risk 3: Sensor drift, calibration errors, or environmental interference (e.g., GNSS signal loss) could invalidate productivity measurements.
Risk 4: Safety interlocks may be too restrictive, preventing operators from executing necessary maneuvers and reducing productivity below target.
Risk 5: Operator training and familiarization with remote control may require extended ramp-up time, affecting productivity during the evaluation period.
Risk 6: Measurement subsystem ownership and implementation are not yet assigned to a specific module; data integrity and traceability may be at risk.
evidence_needed: Evidence 1: Vehicle performance baseline (cycle time, payload capacity, fuel consumption) from manufacturer or prototype testing.
Evidence 2: Teleoperation network performance (latency, bandwidth, reliability) from field trials or simulation.
Evidence 3: Sensor accuracy and calibration procedures from sensor manufacturers and integration testing.
Evidence 4: Safety constraint specifications and enforcement logic from safety analysis (FMEA, FTA) and regulatory requirements.
Evidence 5: Operator training plan and proficiency assessment criteria for remote operation.
Evidence 6: Representative task definitions and worksite conditions for the evaluation period.
Evidence 7: Measurement subsystem design (data acquisition, transmission, storage, analysis) and data integrity assurance plan.
